subject: Sports
title: Nadia Comaneci: Youngest Olympic Gymnastics Gold
description: One-minute skill: Nadia Comaneci: Youngest Olympic Gymnastics Gold.

hook.text: She was 14 and she was perfect. Nadia Comaneci won three golds at the 1976 Olympics before turning 15.

buildup.text: At Montreal, Comaneci was tiny, focused, and impossibly precise. Her routines left judges no room for deduction.

discovery.text: She won gold in the all-around, uneven bars, and balance beam—all at age 14.

twist.text: Modern gymnastics has since raised the minimum Olympic age to 16, making her record permanently out of reach.

climax.text: Her youth was both her advantage—fearlessness—and her legacy. No one that young will ever compete again.

punchline.text: Fourteen and flawless. The rules changed so no one could follow her.

quiz.question: Why can no one break Comaneci's age record at the Olympics?
options: The minimum age for Olympic gymnastics was raised to 16
She holds the record for most perfect scores
The Olympic Committee retired her records
